description_long: This is a classic biplane with a robust fuselage and two sets of straight, parallel wings stacked one above the other, connected by struts. The airplane features a rounded nose with a visible propeller and a radial engine, and the cockpit is situated just behind the upper wing, enclosed with a clear canopy. The tail section includes a single vertical stabilizer and a horizontal stabilizer, both with rounded edges. The landing gear is fixed, with two main wheels under the wings and a smaller tailwheel at the rear. The exterior is primarily white with red accents on the wingtips, tail, and along the fuselage, and subtle blue highlights around the cockpit area. The overall design suggests a vintage or utility aircraft, suitable for short takeoffs and landings in urban or rural environments.
description: A vintage-style biplane with white and red coloring, featuring two stacked wings, a radial engine with a propeller, and a fixed landing gear. The cockpit is enclosed and positioned just behind the upper wing.
description_view_0: Oblique side view showing the radial engine, propeller, and the stacked biplane wings with red accents.
description_view_1: Rear view highlighting the twin wings, single vertical stabilizer, and the fixed landing gear.
description_view_2: Opposite oblique side view, displaying the tailwheel, cockpit canopy, and the full length of the fuselage.
description_view_3: Frontal view showing the propeller, engine cowling, and the symmetrical biplane wing structure.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: High-wing biplane configuration with two straight, parallel wings connected by vertical struts; no winglets or pronounced dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_engine_type: Single front-mounted piston engine with a visible propeller.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Fixed taildragger landing gear with two main wheels and a single tailwheel.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed cockpit located just behind the upper wing, with a clear canopy and likely single or tandem seating.
